title: Day of the Week Converter
description: Convert a day number to the day name using a switch statement!
instructions:
Write a program that converts a day number (1-7) to the day name:

1. Create an int variable 'dayNumber' and set it to any value 1-7
2. Use a switch statement to display:
   - 1 → 'Monday'
   - 2 → 'Tuesday'
   - 3 → 'Wednesday'
   - 4 → 'Thursday'
   - 5 → 'Friday'
   - 6 → 'Saturday'
   - 7 → 'Sunday'
   - default → 'Invalid day number'

3. Try different values to test all cases!

testCases.description: Output should contain a day name or 'Invalid'

hints.text: Each case looks like: case 1: Console.WriteLine("Monday"); break;
Don't forget the 'break;' after each case - it's required in C#!
End with a 'default:' case to handle numbers outside 1-7
Alternative: Try rewriting using a switch expression: string dayName = dayNumber switch { 1 => "Monday", ... };

commonMistakes.consequence: The code won't compile! C# requires break to prevent accidental fall-through.
commonMistakes.correction: Always add break; (or return/throw) at the end of each case.

commonMistakes.consequence: case "1" won't match int 1 - type mismatch!
commonMistakes.correction: Make sure your case types match your switch variable type.

commonMistakes.consequence: Invalid inputs won't be handled gracefully.
commonMistakes.correction: Always include a default case for unexpected values.
